obligations.reason: The debug module (DM) bus master may issue read/write requests to any peripheral or memory without being gated by the access-control layer (acc_ctrl) or register-lock layer (reglk_ctrl). A missing guard would allow an external debugger to access protected assets even when the system requires privilege or lock checks.
obligations.subject: debug module (DM) AXI bus master
obligations.object: all peripherals and memory regions accessible via the NoC
obligations.expected_guard: access-control permission check (acc_ctrl_c) or register-lock check (reglk_ctrl) before allowing bus transactions; at minimum, the DM should be forced to obey the same privilege/lock rules as the CPU
obligations.candidate_path: from dm_master_req/dm_master_add/dm_master_we/dm_master_wdata through the AXI fabric to target peripherals (e.g., via arian_rst_buf_noc*, arian_aes0_buf_noc*, etc.)
state_conditions_to_check: priv_lvl_i = '00 (user mode) while the CPU would be blocked
lock bits for the target peripheral are set in reglk_ctrl
required_evidence_slots: object: specific protected register or memory
observed behavior: transaction proceeds without check
path: from dm_master_req to target peripheral
impact: unauthorised read/write of secure state
source locations: riscv_peripherals.sv (dm_master_* signal assignment and NoC connection)
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows dm_master_* signals but does not reveal whether an access-control crossbar sits between the DM master and the NoC. Analysis must trace dm_master_req to its final destination to confirm no guard is present.

obligations.reason: JTAG-based debug/DMI access may reach internal buses (via SimDTM, dmi_jtag, SimJTAG) without requiring prior authentication. If the JTAG unlock mechanism (jtag_unlock) can be bypassed or is not enforced, an attacker with JTAG connectivity could access protected state.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug user (through i_SimDTM, i_dmi_jtag, i_SimJTAG)
obligations.operation: read/write via DMI to system bus
obligations.object: internal SoC state (fuses, crypto registers, boot ROM, etc.)
obligations.expected_guard: authentication complete and jtag_unlock asserted, or debug access gate kept disabled until authentication
obligations.candidate_path: from JTAG TAP signals (tck, tms, tdi, tdo) through dmi_jtag to debug module (dm_slave_*) and then to the bus fabric
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_hash register contents (if authentication relies on hash)
debug_mode_i may or may not be set
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: access allowed with jtag_unlock==0
path: dmi_jtag to dm_slave_* to dm_master_*
source locations: riscv_peripherals.sv (JTAG instances, jtag_unlock signal)
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows jtag_unlock and jtag_hash but not how they gate the debug path. Analysis must verify whether dm_slave_req or dm_master_req is disabled when jtag_unlock is low.

obligations.reason: The testmode_i input may place the design in a manufacturing test state that bypasses normal access controls (acc_ctrl_c, reglk_ctrl) or forces reset/override signals. If testmode allows unfettered access, it could be used to read/write protected state after deployment.
obligations.subject: test interface (testmode_i)
obligations.operation: enable test mode and perform bus accesses or direct register writes
obligations.object: any access-controlled register, lock bit, or secret
obligations.expected_guard: testmode should not override security-related locks or access permissions; ideally, testmode should be permanently disabled after manufacturing via fuses or lifecycle state
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i signal propagates into modules rist_peripherals, reglk_wrapper, acct_wrapper, etc.
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: access still blocked by lock/acc_ctrl
impact: bypass of all access controls
source locations: multiple modules where testmode_i appears
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows testmode_i as an input but does not show how it is used internally. Analysis must grep for testmode_i to see if it gates any security checks.

obligations.reason: The boot ROM read path is guarded by `rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]`, which uses the CPU privilege level. A second initiator (e.g., debug module, test, or external host via NoC) that can assert rom_req_acct independently could bypass this privilege check and read the ROM content.
obligations.subject: any master that can drive rom_req_acct high
obligations.expected_guard: only authorised privilege levels (machine mode) may read the ROM, and no alternative path that ignores acc_ctrl_c should exist
obligations.candidate_path: rom_req_acct signal sourced from outside the shown module (likely NoC); if driven directly by debug, test, or any bus master, the privilege check may be bypassed
state_conditions_to_check: rom_req_acct is asserted by non-CPU master
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug module or external NoC master
observed behavior: ROM read proceeds while acc_ctrl_c gate is low
path: rom_req_acct to boot ROM instances
impact: leak of boot ROM secrets
source locations: riscv_peripherals.sv (rom_req_acct, acc_ctrl_c, boot ROM instances)
obligations.uncertainty: rom_req_acct is a signal whose driver is not fully shown; its source must be traced to see if debug or test can assert it independently.

obligations.reason: Fuse values (e.g., device keys) are read via `fuse_req`, `fuse_addr`, `fuse_rdata`. If fuse reads are not restricted to a trusted state (e.g., machine mode, secure boot, or after lock), an unprivileged agent or debugger could extract secret key material.
obligations.subject: any bus master or debug interface that can trigger a fuse read
obligations.expected_guard: fuse read enable must require machine-mode privilege, lock state, or debug exclusion; additionally, the fuse read path should be one-time or protected after boot
obligations.candidate_path: from a bus interface to `fuse_req`; the fact layer shows `fuse_req` and `fuse_addr`/`fuse_rdata` signals, but not their gating condition
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit for fuse access not set in reglk_ctrl
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege check or lock
observed behavior: read succeeds without guard
state conditions: user mode, debug active
obligations.uncertainty: The gating logic for fuse_req is not visible in the fact layer. Analysis must locate the source of fuse_req and verify permission checks.

obligations.reason: The reglk_wrapper outputs a lock-control vector (reglk_ctrl_o) that should block writes to specific peripheral registers when the lock bit is set. If any write path (e.g., from CPU, DMA, debug) to those registers does not consult the lock vector, the lock can be bypassed.
obligations.subject: any initiator capable of writing to a peripheral that is under lock control
obligations.object: any peripheral register mapped via reglk_wrapper (e.g., in AES, HMAC, RST controllers)
obligations.expected_guard: writes must be suppressed when the corresponding reglk_ctrl lock bit is set, regardless of initiator type
obligations.candidate_path: through reglk_wrapper: from 'ariane_axi' inputs to 'ariane_axi' outputs, controlled by address decode and reglk_ctrl comparison
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: write enable gated by lock bit
observed behavior: write proceeds when lock=1
impact: unauthorized modification of protected configuration
obligations.uncertainty: The exact implementation of reglk_wrapper is not fully detailed; analysis must confirm that write enable is indeed gated by the lock bit on every write path, including debug and test paths.

obligations.reason: The reset controller (rst_wrapper) accepts debug_mode_i, reglk_ctrl_i, and acct_ctrl_i. If debug mode can force peripheral resets even when the peripheral is locked (reglk_ctrl prevents writes), an attacker could reset a crypto engine mid-operation or clear security state.
obligations.subject: debug mode or test mode
obligations.expected_guard: peripheral reset should be gated by lock status; if a peripheral is locked, only a full system reset (or higher authority) should reset it, and debug mode should not override the lock
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i influences rst_wrapper to assert one of the rst_* outputs; this could bypass the lock check that normally prevents writes to the peripheral's configuration registers
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit for target peripheral set in reglk_ctrl_i
acct_ctrl_i may also indicate write protection
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: reset asserted despite lock
impact: security bypass via forced reset
obligations.uncertainty: The rst_wrapper logic is not fully described; analysis must examine the internal state machine (start, rst_id, rst_mem) to see if lock bits influence reset generation.

obligations.reason: Crypto accelerators (AES, SHA-256, HMAC) could have internal state registers that are readable via debug or test paths to extract key material or intermediate data. Although no direct read mux is visible in the fact layer, the modules' bus interfaces (e.g., arian_aes0_buf_noc3_data_o) may expose internal state if not properly limited.
obligations.subject: debug/test access or any bus master via read path of crypto peripheral
obligations.operation: read internal state registers of crypto module
obligations.object: internal crypto state (round keys, plaintext buffers, intermediate hash values)
obligations.expected_guard: Read operations to crypto registers should be disabled when the engine is not in a safe idle state, or registers should be write-only; any debug path to internal state must be removed
obligations.candidate_path: from AXI read channel to crypto module's rdata output; alternatively, debug may read the registers directly through SoC-level scan chains
state_conditions_to_check: reglk_ctrl lock bit for crypto module is set
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: read blocked when lock set or engine busy
observed behavior: read returns internal state
path: read data path from crypto accelerator to NoC
source locations: crypto wrappers (not listed in fact layer) and riscv_peripherals.sv
obligations.uncertainty: The internal structure of crypto modules is not provided; analysis must check whether the read interface to those modules ever returns internal state and whether debug can access the same registers.

obligations.reason: A DMA engine (connected via buf_ariane_dma_noc2_*) may be able to read/write physical addresses without being subject to the same PMP or access-control rules as the CPU. If the DMA can access protected memory (e.g., containing keys or security policies) while in user mode, it would bypass CPU privilege checks.
obligations.subject: DMA engine (the instance connected to the DMA NoC ports)
obligations.object: any memory or MMIO region that is access-controlled for the CPU
obligations.expected_guard: the DMA should only access regions allowed by a per-channel or global permission table (e.g., set of address ranges, protection bits) that is configured securely
obligations.candidate_path: from DMA request to the NoC and onward to the target; the fact layer shows buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i and ariane_dma_buf_noc2_ready_o, indicating a two-way NoC port
state_conditions_to_check: DMA configuration registers (not shown) set to an unprivileged range
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: address-based access control (PMP or similar)
observed behavior: transfer proceeds beyond allowed range
path: DMA AXI bus to target
state conditions: DMA config set to protected area
obligations.uncertainty: The DMA controller's register interface is not shown; analysis must check whether the DMA address space is constrained by the system address map or by an explicit protection unit.

obligations.reason: The CLINT (timer and IPI) registers are accessible via an AXI-lite interface. If writes to these registers are not restricted to machine mode, unprivileged software could reprogram timers, trigger spurious inter-processor interrupts, or cause denial of service.
obligations.subject: any AXI master (CPU, debug, DMA) that can access the CLINT AXI-lite interface
obligations.operation: write to CLINT registers (mtimecmp, mtime, msip, etc.)
obligations.expected_guard: privilege check: only machine-mode writes should be accepted; debug mode should be prevented from writing if the security policy requires it
obligations.candidate_path: from AXI bus (ariane_axi) through axi_lite_interface to CLINT internal registers
state_conditions_to_check: privilege mode is user or supervisor
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege check or lock
observed behavior: write succeeds without privilege
obligations.uncertainty: The axi_lite_interface only translates AXI signals; privilege enforcement must happen either in the CLINT module or in the wrapper; analysis must check the CLINT's internal register access policies.

obligations.reason: The PLIC (Platform-Level Interrupt Controller) has configuration registers for priorities and enables. These registers should be writable only from machine mode to prevent unprivileged software from rerouting or disabling interrupts.
obligations.subject: any bus master accessing the PLIC AXI-lite interface
obligations.candidate_path: from AXI bus (buf_ariane_plic_noc2_*) through the PLIC interface
obligations.uncertainty: Same as CLINT; need to check the PLIC internal access policy.

obligations.reason: The we_flag_0..4 inputs and the acc_ctrl matrix control write enable to peripherals. If any of these signals can be asserted independently of privilege/lock by a co-processor or debug logic, it could create a side-channel write enable path that bypasses the normal access control.
obligations.subject: any entity that can drive we_flag_* or modify acc_ctrl bits
obligations.operation: enable a write to a peripheral
obligations.expected_guard: we_flag_* must be generated solely from legal bus transactions that have passed all privilege and lock checks; no alternative direct drive
obligations.candidate_path: we_flag_0..4 signals are connected from outside riscv_peripherals; if they originate from debug or test controller, they could override acc_ctrl logic
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: flag only set after access control check
observed behavior: flag set without check
obligations.uncertainty: The origin of we_flag_0..4 is not specified; analysis must verify they cannot be asserted by debug/test without permission.
